abstract: Stops the service associated with the specified provider.
overview:
## Return Value

A value that indicates the service-stopping result. Return [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/DriverKit/kIOReturnSuccess] to inidicate success. To indicate a failure, see [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/iokit_constants] for error definitions.

## Discussion

Before terminating the object in `provider`, the system calls this method to stop the service associated with that object. Use your implementation of this method to stop all activity and put your driver in a quiescent state. If your driver has any in-progress asynchronous tasks, cancel those tasks and wait for DriverKit to call the associated cancellation handler before calling the `super` version of this method.

Call `super` at the end of your implementation. After calling `super`, it’s a programmer error to access the provider object.

Don’t use this method to release your `ivars` structure; use the [doc://com.apple.blockstoragedevicedriverkit/documentation/BlockStorageDeviceDriverKit/IOUserBlockStorageDevice/free] method instead.

# Stop

**Instance Method**

Stops the service associated with the specified provider.

## Declaration

```swift
virtual kern_return_t Stop(IOService *provider);
```

## Parameters

- **provider**: The provider associated with the current service. This object is the same one that the system previously passed to your service’s [doc://com.apple.blockstoragedevicedriverkit/documentation/BlockStorageDeviceDriverKit/IOUserBlockStorageDevice/Start] method.

## Return Value

A value that indicates the service-stopping result. Return [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/DriverKit/kIOReturnSuccess] to inidicate success. To indicate a failure, see [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/iokit_constants] for error definitions.

## Discussion

Before terminating the object in `provider`, the system calls this method to stop the service associated with that object. Use your implementation of this method to stop all activity and put your driver in a quiescent state. If your driver has any in-progress asynchronous tasks, cancel those tasks and wait for DriverKit to call the associated cancellation handler before calling the `super` version of this method.

Call `super` at the end of your implementation. After calling `super`, it’s a programmer error to access the provider object.

Don’t use this method to release your `ivars` structure; use the [doc://com.apple.blockstoragedevicedriverkit/documentation/BlockStorageDeviceDriverKit/IOUserBlockStorageDevice/free] method instead.

## Running the Service

- **init**: Handles the basic initialization of the service.
- **Start**: Starts the current service and associates it with the specified provider.
- **free**: Performs any final cleanup for the service.
